How to use this tab
Decide whether the comparison is clinically usable.
1
Confirm conditions, medications, and labs look correct.
2
Check whether the matched group is balanced.
3
Read similar-case outcomes only after match quality.
Match Quality Check
Are the comparison patients close enough to trust?
Similar-Case Outcomes
What happened to patients who looked clinically similar?
Percentages may not sum to 100 due to rounding.
Adjusted Outcome Signal
After adjusting for patient and clinical differences.
Trajectories shown, not predictions. Use alongside clinical judgment.
How to use this tab
Look for when similar patients began to worsen or recover.
1
Scan the event-free curve for large drops.
2
Compare favorable vs concerning paths.
3
Use node size and arrows to read likely transitions.
Event-Free Timeline
Kaplan-Meier style view of time until hospitalization or deterioration.
Likely State Transitions
A plain-language summary of common movement between clinical states.
How to use this tab
Pick the clinical factors and comparison features to verify first.
1
Start with the strongest risk driver.
2
Review the dominant similar-patient pattern.
3
Check cohort differences before opening cases.
Risk Drivers to Review
The clearest factors pushing this patient toward a concerning trajectory.
Similar-Patient Patterns
Which clinical paths dominate among the matched cases?
Patient vs Similar Cohort
Where this patient aligns with, or differs from, the comparison group.
How to use this tab
Choose which matched patient story to review first.
1
Use the outcome filter to narrow the list.
2
Open the highest-match case.
3
Read why it matched and the turning point.
